celebrations/the-festival-of-legends.htm.title: The Festival of Legends
celebrations/the-festival-of-legends.htm.description:
The Festival of Legends is a long-standing festival procured for the celebration of The Legends of Faeria and their mighty exploits during The Classical Era. It is celebrated near the end of Autumn, accompanied by colorful leaves atop Faeria's forests.
During the Festival, several large parades are thrown throughout Faeria, and areas of certain districts are host to a plethora of celebratory stalls, shows, and speeches. These events are primarily managed by the Government of Faeria, but citizens of Faeria have been known to produce their own stalls and performances, which often earn popularity from other citizens.

celebrations/trials-of-glory.html.title: Trials of Glory
celebrations/trials-of-glory.html.description:
The Trials of Glory is a kingdom-wide tournament hosted in The Kingdom of Marcell every two summers. Prominent individuals across Haera are often invited to spectate, and often adventurers from all sorts of places converge to the Trials for a chance to compete and win the grand prize, consisting of an audience from the Monarch and the granting of a feasible wish. The winners of the Trials usually receive an additional 10,000 gold total.
The Trials often contain a sequence of stages and phases, where teams contend against each other or work together to progress to the next stage. In the past, grandiose battles against monsters or other teams have been portrayed as highlighted spectacles, and often times formidable creatures or beasts are wrangled from treacherous lands by the elites of the Kinsguard to facilitate the most anticipated final stage. The Trials mostly take place in the Aquarée Colosseum, which is home to a legendary artefact that outputs a protective barrier around the venue, preventing humanoids from actual death.

characters/non-playable-characters/acade.title: Dilor Griffin
characters/non-playable-characters/acade.description: Dilor Griffin is the Dwarf leader of the College of Mechanical Studies. Throughout his years at the College, he has crafted numerous outstanding and unique mechanical devices and weaponry, earning his grand reputation in the College and eventually becoming its leader.

characters/non-playable-characters/acade.title: Forbur Wilkinberry
characters/non-playable-characters/acade.description: Forbur Wilkinberry is a Gnome professor in The School of Ego who is regarded as one of the greatest wizard academics of The Modern Era, having invented numerous items that store and contain magic, and pioneered the development and theory of Stored Magic along with his wife Seradine Blackwood.

characters/non-playable-characters/acade.title: Kahl Czekh
characters/non-playable-characters/acade.description: Kahl Czech is a renowned Dragonborn professor and instructor at the Institute of Natural Sciences. He is the pioneer in leading developments and projects in natural sciences across Haera, and was responsible for Thawnia's acceptance of the Institute taking base within it.

characters/non-playable-characters/acade.title: Seradine Blackwood
characters/non-playable-characters/acade.description: Seradine Blackwood is the wife of Forbur Wilkinberry, and a highly renowned Human Sorcerer in the The School of Ego. She pioneered the development and theory of Stored Magic with her husband, paving the path forwards to attunement-less magic item use.

characters/non-playable-characters/acade.title: Tazmir Avhor
characters/non-playable-characters/acade.description:
Tazmir Avhor is a Tiefling Warlock who has a pact with a Fiend within the Nine Hells. Despite her infernal origins, Avhor has participated in many research studies regarding pact magic, inter-planar energy transfer and inter-planar travel.
Most notably, Avhor was a major contributor to the Conduit Travel Theory, being the most enthusiastic participant who joined the research team to discern potential results at Conduit.

characters/non-playable-characters/battl.title: Alexandra Mezzine
characters/non-playable-characters/battl.description: Alexandra Mezzine is a renowned drow Grand Battlemage of the Haeran Battlemage Association, and a part of The Convergent Six. She is the first mage to fail the 'final test' of the Grand Battlemage test and to later succeed with an accompanying announcement that she will become a part of the Convergent Six. Mezzine is a sorcerer who specializes in powerful spells involved in the warping and manipulation of shadows, as well as the absence of light (notably not equivalent to darkness). Some of her signature moves involve dissolving into shadows, summoning creatures and attacks from shadow, and utilising swift light spells to cast shadows, and once the spell dissolves, the absence of light it momentarily produces can be exploited for other combat techniques.

characters/non-playable-characters/battl.title: Avoir Bezian
characters/non-playable-characters/battl.description: Avoir Bezian is a renowned human Grand Battlemage of the Haeran Battlemage Association, and a part of The Convergent Six.Bezian is a powerful sorcerer known for her ability to 'resonate', which is a fickle and taxing process where she can attempt to attune with the core existence of an object, magical artifact or person. Resonance can only occur once for each type of object or individual person, so there are numerous powerful artifacts or people she has not attempted resonating with yet. Successful resonance allows Bezian to manifest the target's essence into a specific, discrete and unique frequency, which she can call upon in battle by changing the frequency she resonates at. This allows her to access one spell or ability key to the target's essence, and in the case of mages, this often results in her learning their most specialized spell. Although the ability to resonate is powerful, attunement is an exhausting ritual that requires numerous days or even weeks, and many mages of the HBA often decline her advances for resonance. Regardless, she has resonated with many targets throughout her life, granting her formidable battle prowess.

characters/non-playable-characters/battl.title: Linae Svartheim
characters/non-playable-characters/battl.description: Linae Svartheim is a renowned elven Grand Battlemage of the Haeran Battlemage Association, and a part of The Convergent Six.Svartheim is a wise and studious witch, who has read a vast volume of tomes and spell books throughout her life, with the capabilities to speak and read ancient languages and runes that are now extinct. As a result of her long life, Svartheim has amassed a large amount of magical power, which she can reliably call upon to carry out powerful ancient rituals or spells, capable of razing lands or tearing through the fabric of existence.

characters/non-playable-characters/battl.title: Luminar Sundriel
characters/non-playable-characters/battl.description: Luminar Sundriel is a renowned aasimar Grand Battlemage of the Haeran Battlemage Association, and a part of The Convergent Six.Sundriel is a Celestial Patron Warlock, famous for his melee-focused abilities. He has a powerful pact for his weapon, which he can fluently manipulate into different shapes, sizes or materials with holy spells granted by his patron. Though his object manipulation is specialized for his weapon, Sundriel is also capable of harnessing divine energy with his spellcasting, increasing their prowess and strength, while boasting a decent range of clergy spells, such as healing or removing curses.It is rumored that Sundriel's weapon can extend up to 1 mile in length before it becomes taxing for him to maintain, and the flexibility of his weapon allows him to store it as a seemingly harmless ring which he can always call upon in an emergency.

characters/non-playable-characters/battl.title: Viktor Steinsman
characters/non-playable-characters/battl.description:
Viktor Steinsman is a renowned human Grand Battlemage of the Haeran Battlemage Association, and a part of The Convergent Six.
Steinsman is a formidable wizard who has great talent in learning spells from ancient texts, and is extremely fluent in numerous extinct languages, taught by his mentor Linae Svartheim. Steinsman has a preference for spells that animate objects, often manifesting them into fantastic and unconventional creatures for combat. Manipulation of objects with telekinesis is also his specialty, making him an extremely powerful battlemage in areas with lots of inanimate objects or nature.

characters/non-playable-characters/battl.title: Zh'aik Khertz
characters/non-playable-characters/battl.description: Zh'aik Khertz is a renowned dragonborn Grand Battlemage of the Haeran Battlemage Association, and a part of The Convergent Six.Khertz is a Great Old One Warlock who has profound prowess and control in harnessing eldritch energy from his patron. He claims he is able to project his connection with his patron onto a selection of other slumbering beings, creating a network of magical power and energy for him to use.He is famous for his unthinkably large pool of mana and spell power, seemingly endless. As a result, he is well-known for an impenetrable combat style due to his vast well of defensive and offensive abilities.

characters/non-playable-characters/heroe.title: Gimmel the Everbrook Hero
characters/non-playable-characters/heroe.description: Gimmel the Everbrook Hero was a regional Human hero in The Classical Era, supporting and protecting Central Haera, primarily Everbrook and Anecdote. He was part of a party with a mage, cleric and fighter, but their names are unknown.It is rumored that Gimmel originally planned to tour the world to save it from monsters, but the King of Anecdote at the time rejected their application for funds. Gimmel settled for smaller-scale adventures, and slew many formidable foes near the Central Haera region, such as a red dragon and a hydra.Statues commemorating him and be found around Everbrook, but many of his stories have faded over the long course of history. His tomb lies between Forager and Brooksby of Everbrook, with carved walls that depict his heroic acts in life.
characters/non-playable-characters/heroe.sourcePath: Characters/Non Playable Characters/Heroes/Gimmel the Everbrook Hero.md

characters/non-playable-characters/heroe.title: Strahtem the Orbiter
characters/non-playable-characters/heroe.description:
Strahtem the Orbiter was an extremely powerful and legendary elven sorcerer during The Warring Times, supported by his party of four other members: Gin'thrak the Githyanki Fighter, Morhus the Goliath Barbarian, Hyriahh the Halfling Rogue and Ferno, the holy Tiefling witch. Strahtem specialized in a unique and dangerous spell that could categorize arbitrary targets into astrological orders, and compel them to rotate about a pre-defined orbit characterized by the natures of each target, their distance relative to each other, and the astrological order chosen by Strahtem. He utilized this spell along with a powerful domain that enforces non-violence, forcing his targets to resolve his orbital spell before attempting to attack him. Targets unable to identify the orbit and resolve it within the orbital period of the astrological order will be thrown into gravitational collapse, and be compressed into a dense, extra-dimensional point in space.
His complex technique and clever combination of powers enabled him to defeat a vast range of human and monstrous enemies, including Villains of his era and a handful of Ancient Dragons. Nowadays, his remains lie within an isolated tomb in Ergo.

characters/non-playable-characters/heroe.title: The Dunewalker Ashtul
characters/non-playable-characters/heroe.description: The Dunewalker Ashtul was a Dragonborn Paladin originating from a tribe in Drakehidt, famed for her achievements in Southern Haera during The Warring Times. She and her party of 5 others travelled and toiled through the relentless heat of the Egia Desert and Greyhorde, fending off ravenous beasts and was rumored to have explored a subsection of the Blighted Lands, but currently there is no evidence of this.

characters/non-playable-characters/heroe.title: The Legends of Faeria
characters/non-playable-characters/heroe.description:
The Legends of Faeria were a party of powerful individuals primarily based in Faeria during The Classical Era. Their formidable exploits across Haera and other continents were greatly celebrated and revered at the time, and Faeria still celebrates The Festival of Legends in The Modern Era, despite the extreme passage of time since then. The party consisted of the following people:
Lorawynn the Elf Bard and party leader. Rumors say she tamed a dragon by seducing it.
Valena the Aasimar Monk. It is said that she once bargained with the gods and convinced them to lay waste to a never-before-seen horde of horrific monsters invading from another plane.
Wilston the Gnome Wizard. It was said that he was so skilled with magic that he could conjure a gigantic mechanical construct to fight alongside the party.
Rygor the Human Druid. It was rumored that he could transform up to 72 wild shapes. The party is buried under a well respected and highly protected temple in Faeria.
characters/non-playable-characters/heroe.sourcePath: Characters/Non Playable Characters/Heroes/The Legends of Faeria.md

characters/non-playable-characters/heroe.title: The Northwind Wanderer
characters/non-playable-characters/heroe.description: The Northwind Wanderer was a regional Goliath hero during The Classical Era. He was largely spotted across small settlements and villagers across Northwind, aiding Upper Central Haera against routine or sudden threats emerging around Schmine and the forests. Though he often travelled alone, it is rumored that he led a peaceful life of retirement and it is speculated that some of his descendants may still be roaming Northwind to this day.

characters/non-playable-characters/monar.title: Amelia Lukeheart
characters/non-playable-characters/monar.description:
Amelia Lukeheart was a prominent and famed Human Queen of The Kingdom of Marcell during The Classical Era. Amelia passed peacefully at the age of 66, handing the crown to her son Vasir.
She introduced the Kinsguard to Marcell, and was the first Queen of Marcell since The Forgotten Times.

characters/non-playable-characters/monar.title: Arthur Lukeheart
characters/non-playable-characters/monar.description: Arthur Lukeheart (not to be confused with King Arthur Lukeheart II) was the Human king of The Kingdom of Marcell and died at age 72, being defeated by an evil dragon later slain by his son, King Arthur Lukeheart II. In life, Lukeheart occasionally had bursts of prophetic vision, which has influenced his ruling over the course of his kingship. Citizens were generally amiable towards his use of his strange power, and did not attempt to thwart his reign as he was a wise king whose tactical and strategic knowledge was greatly valued in battle, and was considerably generous to the common people.

characters/non-playable-characters/monar.title: Juan Alonzo
characters/non-playable-characters/monar.description:
"He radiated celestial light to pave the path for others throughout his orbit. May he now rest in peace." Queen Masja Alonzo in the public funeral of Juan Alonzo.
Juan Alonzo was the Aasimar king of Abstract and died at age 54 during a battle near Landspine. In life, Alonzo was a strong ruler who was fair and just. He was a powerful Paladin, but lacked inventive creativity, hence did not participate in the proceedings of the House of Alonzo.

characters/non-playable-characters/monar.title: King Arthur Lukeheart II
characters/non-playable-characters/monar.description:
"Soon, a great peril will fall upon the Kingdom. Evil incarnate, in the form of a devastating dragon will strike Marcell. It is by defeating this malignant creature that my son will be crowned King." Prophecy by Arthur Lukeheart three years before the incident.
King Arthur Lukeheart II is the current Human king of The Kingdom of Marcell. He is a valiant warrior in battle, having conquered many foes in regional battles mostly around Upper Central Haera. His late father, Arthur Lukeheart deemed his child will be destined for kingship after slaying a mighty dragon, which ultimately occurred at the cost of several lives, including that of his father.
characters/non-playable-characters/monar.sourcePath: Characters/Non Playable Characters/Monarchs/King Arthur Lukeheart II.md

characters/non-playable-characters/monar.title: Queen Masja Alonzo
characters/non-playable-characters/monar.description:
Queen Masja Alonzo is the current Aasimar monarch of Abstract. Before she became Queen, she was a notable inventor in the House of Alonzo, developing many trinkets and devices helpful to the general public.
She was a prevalent council member of the Scholars of Thought, and had to step down after the passing of her brother Juan Alonzo three years ago. She is an amiable leader and members of Abstract are content with her ruling.

characters/non-playable-characters/monar.title: Vasir Lukeheart
characters/non-playable-characters/monar.description: Vasir Lukeheart was a Human king of The Kingdom of Marcell during The Classical Era. He was assassinated at age 47 during his reign after he caused numerous incidents that outraged his people and plummeted the reputation of the Kinsguard, who he bribed and used to selectively protect particular cities within Marcell, leaving the rest to fend for themselves against devastating attacks and raids.

characters/non-playable-characters/other.title: Borf Family
characters/non-playable-characters/other.description: The Borf Family is a family of dwarves in Whisp who own, manage and run Winery's Whisp. It has extensive trade connections with the Seaside Trading Guild and the Adventurer's Guild, supplying wines and brews for numerous eras.

characters/non-playable-characters/other.title: Chardine Suave
characters/non-playable-characters/other.description: Chardine Suave is a Halfling who founded Hero's Feast in Hero's Hilt. He was a wonderful chef whose spectacular culinary expertise was praised for many years, even after his passing.

characters/non-playable-characters/peopl.title: Elaine Ridgewater
characters/non-playable-characters/peopl.description:
Elaine Ridgewater is the current owner and leader of the Seaside Trading Guild. She is a Halfling based in Altrae and is rarely seen outside of the Seaside Trading Guildhall. It is said that she orchestrates some of the most influential and important trades in the city of Altrae, and may be involved in numerous dealings in nearby regions.
Her second-in-command is Gor Schmidt, who can often be seen alongside her during big trade discussions and transactions.
characters/non-playable-characters/peopl.sourcePath: Characters/Non Playable Characters/People in Power/Elaine Ridgewater.md

characters/non-playable-characters/peopl.title: Gor Schmidt
characters/non-playable-characters/peopl.description: Gor Schmidt is the second-in-command of the Seaside Trading Guild. He is a Human based in Altrae. Though Schmidt works under Elaine Ridgewater, he is seen more often in public areas than her, taking over minor dealings in her place or investigating and recruiting incoming merchants.Schmidt is often seen as a bodyguard for Ridgewater in important transactions, and it is well-known that he was previously an adventurer as a Rogue.
characters/non-playable-characters/peopl.sourcePath: Characters/Non Playable Characters/People in Power/Gor Schmidt.md

characters/non-playable-characters/peopl.title: Hamish Rippletook
characters/non-playable-characters/peopl.description:
Hamish Rippletook is the acting director of the Seaside Trading Guild Guildhall based in Faeria. He is a Gnome. Though Faeria is on the opposite side of Haera to Altrae, Rippletook is a reliable ally of Elaine Ridgewater since before she became the leader of the guild.
Rippletook is often seen performing trade in the public market, protected by bodyguards of The People's Shield.
characters/non-playable-characters/peopl.sourcePath: Characters/Non Playable Characters/People in Power/Hamish Rippletook.md

cities/major-cities/abstract.html.title: Abstract
cities/major-cities/abstract.html.description:
Abstract is a kingdom consisting of smaller towns and cities in Eastern Haera that is located near Renavoir. It is home to many humans, halflings, and other humanoids, and one of the last regions on Haera that is still run by a monarch.
The current monarch is Queen Masja Alonzo, who has reigned for the past three years since the death of her brother Juan Alonzo. Monarchs:
Queen Masja Alonzo (current, The Modern Era)
Juan Alonzo (The Modern Era) Establishments based in Abstract:
College of Bardic Innovation
House of Alonzo

cities/major-cities/altrae.html.title: Altrae
cities/major-cities/altrae.html.description:
Altrae is a city in Eastern Haera that is located near Merphos. It is a coastal city, home to many humans, halflings, and other humanoids. Establishments based in Altrae:
Seaside Trading Guild
Scholars of Thought

cities/major-cities/ego.html.title: Ego
cities/major-cities/ego.html.description:
Ego is a city in Northern Haera to the south of Ergo. It is home to a wide range of humanoids, primarily humans, gnomes, and some dragonborns. Establishments based in Ego:
Fleetfoot Post
Haeran Battlemage Association
Institute of Natural Sciences
Scholars of Thought
The School of Ego

cities/major-cities/faeria.html.title: Faeria
cities/major-cities/faeria.html.description:
Faeria is a coastal city in Western Haera to the north of Lune and west of Raea. It is neighbored by a small forest, and is the largest city in Haera. It is home to humans, halflings, elves and other humanoids. Notable individuals:
The Legends of Faeria (deceased) Establishments based in Faeria:
Adventurer's Guild
Government of Faeria
Haeran Battlemage Association
Scholars of Thought
The People's Shield Celebrations in Faeria:
The Festival of Legends

cities/major-cities/gherra.html.title: Gherra
cities/major-cities/gherra.html.description:
Gherra is a coastal city in Western Haera, to the north of Faeria. It is a prominent seafaring region, and often sends expeditions into the sea in search of distant lands, islands or treasures. Establishments based in Gherra:
Reformed Seafarers

cities/major-cities/hero's-hilt.html.title: Hero's Hilt
cities/major-cities/hero's-hilt.html.description:
Hero's Hilt is a city located in the south-east end of Split. Its name originates from the belief that a mighty hero's blade created the canyon between the mountains of Split. It primarily consists of halflings and is a major hub for the Adventurer's Guild. Establishments based in Hero's Hilt:
Adventurer's Guild
Continental Warrior Association
Haeran Battlemage Association
Hero's Feast

cities/major-cities/thawnia.html.title: Thawnia
cities/major-cities/thawnia.html.description: Thawnia is a city in Northern Haera. It is home to Dragonborns, ...Establishments based in Thawnia:

cities/major-cities/the-kingdom-of-marce.title: The Kingdom of Marcell
cities/major-cities/the-kingdom-of-marce.description:
The Kingdom of Marcell consists of numerous cities in Upper Central Haera to the west of Sandspine. It is one of the last regions on Haera that is still run by a monarch, and hosts the Trials of Glory every two years.
The current monarch is King Arthur Lukeheart II, who has participated in numerous regional battles in the past. Monarchs:
King Arthur Lukeheart II (current, The Modern Era)
Arthur Lukeheart (The Modern Era)
Amelia Lukeheart (The Classical Era)
Vasir Lukeheart (The Classical Era) Establishments based in The Kingdom of Marcell:
Kinsguard

cities/major-cities/whisp.html.title: Whisp
cities/major-cities/whisp.html.description:
Whisp is a city in Upper Central Haera to the west of Conduit. Though not the biggest of cities within Haera, it is one of the only active trading cities with Thawnia. Establishments based in Whisp:
Winery's Whisp

cities/minor-cities/abermath.html.title: Abermath
cities/minor-cities/abermath.html.description: Abermath is a small city in Upper Central Haera. It is a coastal settlement that primarily earns its keep via fishing and seafood trade. Establishments based in Abermath:

cities/minor-cities/anecdote.html.title: Anecdote
cities/minor-cities/anecdote.html.description:
Anecdote is a city in Central Haera, bordering Everbrook to its west. Establishments based in Anecdote:
Midland Scouts

cities/minor-cities/avador.html.title: Avador
cities/minor-cities/avador.html.description: Avador is a mountain city in Northern Haera, specifically, the Frostlands. Establishments based in Avador:

cities/minor-cities/crekar.html.title: Crekar
cities/minor-cities/crekar.html.description: Crekar is a small city in Western Haera. It hosts a small population, with mountains to its north and south.Establishments based in Crekar:

cities/minor-cities/dhor.html.title: Dhor
cities/minor-cities/dhor.html.description: Dhor is a city in Northern Haera. It is located at the northwest corner of the region, and is home to a sizable population.Establishments based in Dhor:

cities/minor-cities/dragontail.html.title: Dragontail
cities/minor-cities/dragontail.html.description: Dragontail is a city in Central Haera to the south of Landspine. Establishments based in Dragontail:

cities/minor-cities/epiphany.html.title: Epiphany
cities/minor-cities/epiphany.html.description: Epiphany is a small city in Northern Haera, to the north of Krag. Its calm, cold and windy weather has attracted many monks across Haera to meditate and train there, and is believed to have the capability of awakening its namesake for those who are truly dedicated to their beliefs and training.Establishments based in Epiphany:

cities/minor-cities/everbrook.html.title: Everbrook
cities/minor-cities/everbrook.html.description:
Everbrook is a group of small towns in Central Haera, bordering Anecdote on its east. Towns: Forager (Northwest) Brookby (Southeast) Notable individuals:
Gimmel the Everbrook Hero (deceased) Establishments situated in Everbrook:
Adventurer's Guild
Fleetfoot Post
Midland Scouts
The People's Shield

cities/minor-cities/garoa.html.title: Garoa
cities/minor-cities/garoa.html.description: Garoa is a small city in Upper Central Haera, located to the south of Thawnmouth. Establishments based in Garoa:

cities/minor-cities/intercept.html.title: Intercept
cities/minor-cities/intercept.html.description: Intercept is a small city in Upper Central Haera, to the north of The Kingdom of Marcell. Establishments based in Intercept:

cities/minor-cities/krag's-keep.html.title: Krag's Keep
cities/minor-cities/krag's-keep.html.description: Krag's Keep is a set of small towns in Northern Haera, located in the south of Krag. It is founded by saints and churches in The Classical Era, originally worshipping and teaching the mysterious and fantastical phenomenon of Krag.Establishments based in Krag's Keep:

cities/minor-cities/nautai.html.title: Nautai
cities/minor-cities/nautai.html.description: Intercept is a small coastal city in Eastern Haera, to the south of Abstract. It focuses mostly on fishing, but also boasts several exotic fruit and bird species, highly coveted by merchants and traders. Establishments based in Abstract:

cities/minor-cities/orhum.html.title: Orhum
cities/minor-cities/orhum.html.description: Orhum is a city in Northern Haera. It is located to the north of Soarre and northwest of Reykja.Establishments based in Orhum:

cities/minor-cities/reykja.html.title: Reykja
cities/minor-cities/reykja.html.description: Reykja is a city in Northern Haera. It is located to the northeast of Soarre and west of Orhum.Establishments based in Reykja:

cities/minor-cities/spurn.html.title: Spurn
cities/minor-cities/spurn.html.description: Spurn is a city in the north of Churn. It is somewhat near the ocean but not a coastal city. Establishments based in Spurn:

cities/minor-cities/vortex.html.title: Vortex
cities/minor-cities/vortex.html.description: Spurn is a city in the east of Churn. It is an exotic destination for most populations of Haera, and is home to numerous exotic and unique species. Establishments based in Vortex:

continents/haera.html.title: Haera
continents/haera.html.description:
Haera is a continent in the Material Plane, home to a diverse range of humanoids, such as humans or elves.
Regions of Haera:
Regions of Haera Cities in Haera:
Major Cities of Haera
Minor Cities of Haera Establishments in Haera:
Establishments of Haera Academic schools in Haera:
Academic Schools of Haera Past Heroes of Haera:
Heroes of Haera

establishments/adventurer's-guild.html.title: Adventurer's Guild
establishments/adventurer's-guild.html.description:
The Adventurer's Guild is a far-reaching establishment that supports adventurers across Haera, primarily based in Faeria. It is responsible for contracting adventurers for jobs and quests, and often provides supplies and/or resources for sale to aid them in their adventures.
Hero's Hilt is also an important contributor for the Guild, and is well-endowed with standard adventurer supplies. Establishments found in:
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Crekar
Dhor
Dragontail
Ego
Everbrook
Faeria
Gherra
Hero's Hilt
Intercept
Orhum
Reykja
The Kingdom of Marcell
Vortex
Whisp

establishments/continental-warrior-assoc.title: Continental Warrior Association
establishments/continental-warrior-assoc.description:
The Continental Warrior Association (CWA) is a prevalent association for warriors of Haera, primarily based in Hero's Hilt. They have smaller establishments across cities in the continent, and generally work alongside local guard forces to protect regions. They may be seen working alongside Monster Hunters, with whom they have good relations.There are various ranks of warriors, which are determined by seasonal or annual tests proctored by higher ranks: First Class Second Class Third Class Fourth Class Fifth Class
Unlike the Haeran Battlemage Association, if someone passes the First Class test they will become a First Class Warrior without doubt or additional tests.

establishments/fleetfoot-post.html.title: Fleetfoot Post
establishments/fleetfoot-post.html.description:
Fleetfoot Post is a postage and delivery service primarily based in Ego. In collaboration with The School of Ego, Fleetfoot has developed magic-assisted postage systems that can produce temporary portals for pigeon carriers using stored magic (not to be confused with the theory of Stored Magic) acquired from low-energy magic crystals.
Fleetfoot's magical postage system has garnered great attention, and is seen as the best postage service in Haera. Establishments found in:
Abermath
Abstract
Altrae
Dhor
Dragontail
Ego
Epiphany
Everbrook
Faeria
Gherra
Hero's Hilt
Intercept
Nautai
Orhum
Reykja
Spurn
Thawnia
The Kingdom of Marcell
Vortex
Whisp

establishments/government-of-faeria.html.title: Government of Faeria
establishments/government-of-faeria.html.description:
Faeria is run by the Government of Faeria. It was first introduced after The Warring Times where the acting monarch, Queen Farlyn Mysth descended the throne to aid war efforts.
In The Modern Era, the government manages establishments across the city, as well as funding The People's Shield and the Adventurer's Guild.Establishments found in:
Faeria

establishments/haeran-battlemage-associa.title: Haeran Battlemage Association
establishments/haeran-battlemage-associa.description:
The Haeran Battlemage Association (HBA) is an association primarily for battle mages in Haera, based in Aethar. It holds a large number of magical artifacts, tomes and items in extra-planar vaults. Further detail is not authorized to be published by the association.There are four ranks for members of the HBA, which are determined by seasonal or annual tests held by higher-rank proctors in their establishments. The ranks are (from highest to lowest): Grand Battlemage Spellwarden Runebinder Initiate
The tests for Grand Battlemage are held by renowned Grand Battlemages, and there is a mysterious 'final test' that successful candidates must pass in order to ascend to that role. Otherwise, they are rejected and must remain a Spellwarden. It is possible to be rejected at this stage and pass it in a later test, shown by several mages in past records. This test is not present in the Continental Warrior Association.
Notable Individuals (The Convergent Six):
Alexandra Mezzine
Avoir Bezian
Linae Svartheim
Luminar Sundriel
Viktor Steinsman
Zh'aik Khertz Establishments found in:
Aethar
Ego
Faeria
Hero's Hilt

establishments/hero's-feast.html.title: Hero's Feast
establishments/hero's-feast.html.description:
Hero's Feast is a Halfling tavern chain primarily based in Hero's Hilt, founded during The Classical Era by Chardine Suave. Signature dishes: Grilled giant chicken's leg and titan potato soup Deluxe steak and titan potato fries Cheesy lobster and prawn (coastal establishments only) Hero's Feast (Hero's Hilt only) salads procured from regional vegetables titan potato soup multiple species of bird and land animals served in various techniques: grilled, fried, baked with titan potato fries
exotic fruits from Renavoir and Intrigue. Hero's Feast locations:
Hero's Hilt
Abstract
Altrae
Faeria
The Kingdom of Marcell

establishments/house-of-alonzo.html.title: House of Alonzo
establishments/house-of-alonzo.html.description:
The House of Alonzo is a prevalent House in Abstract, but despite its ties to the Abstract monarchy, is a well-established and respected organization that researches and develops mechanical devices, with members from the Scholars of Thought, and primarily Dwarf, Halfling, Human and Aasimar members.
The House also supervises the College of Mechanical Studies, but its leader Dilor Griffin retains ultimate management of the College. Notable members:
Queen Masja Alonzo
Juan Alonzo (deceased) Establishments found in:
Abstract

establishments/kinsguard.html.title: Kinsguard
establishments/kinsguard.html.description:
The Kinsguard (not Kingsguard) is a guard force primarily trained and based in The Kingdom of Marcell. It was first introduced by Queen Amelia Lukeheart during The Classical Era.
Though not strictly bound to The Kingdom of Marcell, the Kinsguard usually exclusively operates to protect the citizens of Marcell from monstrous threats. Seldomly, the Kinsguard may be deployed to protect important carriages as they journey across Haera. They are also enlisted to protect Abermath and Intercept.Establishments found in:
Abermath
Intercept
The Kingdom of Marcell

establishments/midland-scouts.html.title: Midland Scouts
establishments/midland-scouts.html.description:
The Midland Scouts are a small guard and scout force primarily deployed in Anecdote and Everbrook. They also have an established station in The Kingdom of Marcell, though the Kinsguard is much more prevalent there.Establishments found in:
Anecdote
Everbrook
The Kingdom of Marcell

establishments/monastery-of-being.html.title: Monastery of Being
establishments/monastery-of-being.html.description: The Monastery of Being is based in the foot of the five mountains of Forsaken Perch, home to hermits and monks alike who stay there to train and improve themselves. It is open to many divine ideas, and monks of varying specialties find their enlightenment here.The Monastery is open to visitors, but they must not intrude or disrupt the flow and peace of the site.

establishments/monastery-of-the-cloud.ht.title: Monastery of the Cloud
establishments/monastery-of-the-cloud.ht.description: The Monastery of the Cloud is a monastery built in Intrigue, with dedicated monks studying and honing their craft within it. It is open to visitors, but they must be respectful and must not disrupt the peace and flow of the monastery.The Monastery prides itself in providing a spiritual conduit for refining oneself and training oneself to eventually achieve true enlightenment.

establishments/monster-hunters.html.title: Monster Hunters
establishments/monster-hunters.html.description: The Monster Hunters are a faction of warriors, fighters or hunters who have banded together to fight and defeat monsters across Haera. They have close relations with the Continental Warrior Association, and may work together on missions in some regions.The Hunters are focused on tracking, locating and eliminating various fantastical beasts, with a non-strict policy for members who wish to join.

establishments/reformed-seafarers.html.title: Reformed Seafarers
establishments/reformed-seafarers.html.description:
The Reformed Seafarers is a sea exploration organization based in Gherra. It was founded and currently led by a majority of reformed pirates and sea looters, with some hailing from distant lands. They spearhead ocean exploration and expeditions, and have had numerous bountiful adventures, bringing back remarkable artefacts, treasure or research material. Establishments found in:
Gherra

establishments/seaside-trading-guild.htm.title: Seaside Trading Guild
establishments/seaside-trading-guild.htm.description:
"What's yours can be mine, to exact your price divined." Motto of the Seaside Trading Guild The Seaside Trading Guild is a trading and merchant's guild based in Altrae of Eastern Haera. Though Altrae is on the eastern coast of Haera, the guild is very active and prominent in Western Haera, especially the coastal cities Faeria and Gherra. The Seaside Trading Guild is known to operate generously with merchants and traders in cities across Haera, and often rents guildhall spaces to other local guilds, such as local mason, leatherworker, farmer and fishermen guilds. Notable individuals:
Elaine Ridgewater
Gor Schmidt
Hamish Rippletook Establishments found in:
Abermath
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Dhor
Dragontail
Ego
Epiphany
Faeria
Gherra
Hero's Hilt

Vortex

establishments/temples-of-the-divine.htm.title: Temples of the Divine
establishments/temples-of-the-divine.htm.description:
The Temples of the Divine are a collection of prominent and well-respected temples located near Forsaken Perch. They are dedicated to numerous gods, beings or entities, and it is a respectful area where faith and philosophy are openly discussed and exchanged, without judgement. Though numerous temples serve any believers or visitors, some are reserved for specific groups or churches, such as:
The Holy Paladins
The Oathbreakers
The Church of Grace
The Forgotten Church

establishments/the-people's-shield.html.title: The People's Shield
establishments/the-people's-shield.html.description:
The People's Shield is a guard force deployed across all of Haera, but based in Faeria. It aims to protect towns and cities from monster attacks, and regularly patrols across the continent to answer nearby emergencies if required.
The general public appreciate the Shield's protection, and share positive views for the guard force. The Shield is largely funded by the Government of Faeria, but also earns money via dungeon deployments or cross-continent protection work. Establishment found in:
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Dhor
Dragontail
Ego
Everbrook
Faeria
Gherra
Hero's Hilt
Orhum
Reykja
Thawnia
The Kingdom of Marcell
Whisp

establishments/winery's-whisp.html.title: Winery's Whisp
establishments/winery's-whisp.html.description:
Winery's Whisp is a brewery located in Whisp. Though its products are spread far and wide across Haera, the establishment itself remains situated in Whisp. Its founding family, the Borf Family, founded and maintained the core vinery of the company across The Classical Era up till The Modern Era.Establishments found in:
Whisp

history/history-of-haera.html.title: History of Haera
history/history-of-haera.html.description:
The history of Haera is largely defined by four eras, in chronological order: The Forgotten Times
The Classical Era
The Warring Times
The Modern Era

history/the-classical-era.html.title: The Classical Era
history/the-classical-era.html.description:
The Classical Era succeeds The Forgotten Times in the History of Haera, and was a largely stable era where fragmented settlements and groups across Haera began interacting and trading under benign intentions.
Many legendary heroes emerged during this era with mystical weapons and artefacts, fending off demonic beasts, region-level threats and monstrous aberrations with profound might. Many kingdoms and cities flourished and grew under their protection, forming the core foundation of The Modern Era we now know today.Notable heroes of this time:
Gimmel the Everbrook Hero
The Legends of Faeria
The Northwind Wanderer
The Forgotten Times precedes this era, and The Warring Times succeeds it.

history/the-forgotten-times.html.title: The Forgotten Times
history/the-forgotten-times.html.description:
The Forgotten Times is the earliest era in the History of Haera, and only fragmented knowledge of this era is known, due to extensive monster activity in many regions, making it difficult for regions of Haera to communicate with each other. It is thought that only high-ranking families and clans have access to ancestral records referencing potential events and recollections of this era.
Though the Scholars of Thought have repeatedly attempted to acquire information about The Forgotten Times, every known family or clan have turned them down, potentially due to some of the contents presented in such records.
The Classical Era succeeds this era.

history/the-modern-era.html.title: The Modern Era
history/the-modern-era.html.description: The Modern Era is the current era in which Haera lies. It succeeds The Warring Times in the History of Haera.

history/the-warring-times.html.title: The Warring Times
history/the-warring-times.html.description:
The Warring Times was a period of strife and chaos in the History of Haera, succeeding the magnificence of The Classical Era. Numerous kingdom leaders and cities were manipulated or pushed into battle or war across Haera, while monster populations rose due to the decline in emergence of legendary heroes.Warriors and mages during this period specialized heavily in battle and combat, shaping the development and production of magical items or mechanical creations towards more malevolent causes. Though rare, extremely powerful individuals hailing during these times were battle-hardened, savage, and had uncontested strength in their craft.Notable heroes of this time:
Strahtem the Orbiter
The Dunewalker Ashtul
The Modern Era succeeds this era.

knowledge/college-of-bardic-innovation.h.title: College of Bardic Innovation
knowledge/college-of-bardic-innovation.h.description:
The College of Bardic Innovation is a college primarily based in Abstract, dedicated for bards across Haera to learn, improve and refine their craft. It owns several establishments across the continent, but the most prominent bards and performers are usually present in Abstract.Establishments found in:
Abstract
Altrae
Ego
Faeria
Gherra
Hero's Hilt
The Kingdom of Marcell

knowledge/college-of-mechanical-studies..title: College of Mechanical Studies
knowledge/college-of-mechanical-studies..description:
The College of Mechanical Studies is a school for mechanics and tinkerers in Haera. Its current leader is Dilor Griffin. They are based in the city Dragontail. Notable individuals:
Dilor Griffin Establishments found in:
Abstract
Dragontail
Ego
Faeria

knowledge/conduit-travel-theory.html.title: Conduit Travel Theory
knowledge/conduit-travel-theory.html.description:
Conduit Travel Theory (CTT) is a theory based on the magical energy lingering around the mountains of Conduit. Through years of study and research, schools and magic-related groups have arrived to this theory.CTT suggests that the lingering magical energy can be manipulated and 'bound' to certain planes, and this bond can be enhanced with the magical energy of a user. Warlocks have been extensively invited to Conduit for the testing of this theory, due to their inter-planar connections with their patrons, but results are neither confirming nor denying the theory, as there is currently no method to determine whether the bond has been formed.The primary contributors to CTT are:
Tazmir Avhor
The School of Ego
The Circle of Ley Lines
Some may argue that Chromatia could fit into CTT, but this claim is widely disagreed with.

knowledge/institute-of-natural-sciences..title: Institute of Natural Sciences
knowledge/institute-of-natural-sciences..description:
The Institute of Natural Sciences is a well-respected institute that studies and researches magical, non-magical, cursed and benign nature. It is primarily based in Ego, and is one of the only establishments allowed within Thawnia. Notable individuals:
Professor Kahl Czekh Establishments found in:
Ego
Faeria
Thawnia

knowledge/scholars-of-thought.html.title: Scholars of Thought
knowledge/scholars-of-thought.html.description:
The Scholars of Thought are a collection and school of philosophers, historians and theorists well known throughout Haera. The Scholars have extensive reach across the continent, with primary bases in Ego, Faeria and Altrae. Notable individuals:
Queen Masja Alonzo Establishments found in:
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Ego
Faeria
Reykja
The Kingdom of Marcell

knowledge/stored-magic.html.title: Stored Magic
knowledge/stored-magic.html.description:
"A breakthrough in Stored Magic could vastly improve strength and power of guards and armies, greatly heightening their performance against monstrous threats." Professor Forbur Wilkinberry.
Stored magic is a magical theory by Forbur Wilkinberry and Seradine Blackwood that attunement to magic items requires a "magically-tangible" manifestation of the user's soul, thus aligning one's soul to the corresponding item.
Wilkinberry and Blackwood theorised that through capturing and replicating behaviors of a user's soul, it may be possible to duplicate the effect of attunement with magical apparatus, potentially allowing for rapid attunement switching and magic item use in combat scenarios. However, the theory is currently facing opposition in The School of Ego as success in this theory could become very dangerous in the wrong hands.

knowledge/the-circle-of-ley-lines.html.title: The Circle of Ley Lines
knowledge/the-circle-of-ley-lines.html.description:
The Circle of Ley Lines is a small, journeying druidic circle of scholars that aim to define the world via Ley Lines. Though whimsical, traces of the Circle have been observed in numerous of Haera's regions exhibiting more interesting natural phenomenon, such as Canvas and Conduit.
The Institute of Natural Sciences have attempted to locate the Circle after hearing rumors that they originated from Canvas, but has failed to reach them thus far.
The Circle of Ley Lines have contributed to the Conduit Travel Theory, one of the few times the Circle has remained in a specific city for more than a week.

knowledge/the-convergent-six.html.title: The Convergent Six
knowledge/the-convergent-six.html.description:
The Convergent Six are a group of the most powerful battlemages of an era, with the role of Grand Battlemage in the Haeran Battlemage Association. They are the main leaders of the association, and control the highest level of access to the association's vaults and storage areas.They may be replaced only upon the acceptance of a new Grand Battlemage, where the secret 'test' would reveal that the individual is destined for a place in the Six. It also states which mage's position will be replaced. The current members of the Six:
Alexandra Mezzine
Avoir Bezian
Linae Svartheim
Luminar Sundriel
Viktor Steinsman
Zh'aik Khertz
Alexandra Mezzine is the first Grand Battlemage who has failed the mysterious 'final' test, and subsequently passed it with the announcement of her arrival into the six. This, along with existing views of Drow, led to disbelief and jealousy among the HBA, often causing disagreements in meetings.

knowledge/the-school-of-ego.html.title: The School of Ego
knowledge/the-school-of-ego.html.description:
The School of Ego is an established college primarily for wizards and sorcerers, where they work together to formulate magical apparatus, spells and techniques. They are based in the city of Ego in Northern Haera.Notable individuals:
Professor Forbur Wilkinberry
Professor Seradine Blackwood Establishments found in:
Altrae
Ego
Faeria
Hero's Hilt
Reykja
Thawnia

planes/material-plane.html.description:
The material plane is a Plane in the The Material Realms, home to many humanoids, such as humans and elves.
One notable continent in the material plane is Haera.

planes/shadowfell.html.description: The Shadowfell is a Plane in The Material Realms, home to grim wastelands, haunted ruins and the Domains of Dread.

planes/the-material-realms.html.description:

regions/large-regions/central-haera.html.title: Central Haera
regions/large-regions/central-haera.html.description:
Central Haera is a region of lush, green forests accompanied by large hills, mountain ranges, and a marsh, located in the center of Haera.It consists of:
Anecdote
Canvas
Dragontail
Everbrook
Landspine
Split
Stockold

regions/large-regions/eastern-haera.html.title: Eastern Haera
regions/large-regions/eastern-haera.html.description:
Eastern Haera is a region of temperate forests, plains, and a rainforest in the east of Haera. A large desert lies in its north.It consists of:
Abstract
Altrae
Churn
Merphos
Nautai
Renavoir

regions/large-regions/northern-haera.htm.title: Northern Haera
regions/large-regions/northern-haera.htm.description: Northern Haera is a region of snowy plains, forests and mountains in the north of Haera, consisting of the Northern Mountains, Northern Cities and the Frostlands.

regions/large-regions/outer-haera.html.title: Outer Haera
regions/large-regions/outer-haera.html.description:
Outer Haera consists of locations and regions disconnected from the main continent of Haera.It consists of:
Aethar
Blood Frosts
Chromatia
Intrigue
Obsidian Peaks

regions/large-regions/regions-of-haera.h.title: Regions of Haera
regions/large-regions/regions-of-haera.h.description:
A list of large regions in Haera:
Northern Haera
Upper Central Haera
Eastern Haera
Central Haera
Western Haera
Southern Haera
Outer Haera

regions/large-regions/southern-haera.htm.title: Southern Haera
regions/large-regions/southern-haera.htm.description:
Southern Haera is a region largely consisting of deserts, mountains and life-less lands, in the south of Haera.It consists of:
Blighted Lands
Egia Desert
Eror
Forsaken Perch
Greyhorde
Remnant

regions/large-regions/upper-central-haer.title: Upper Central Haera
regions/large-regions/upper-central-haer.description:
Upper Central Haera is a region of cold plains and forests in the northern center of Haera, consisting of:
Abermath
Conduit
Garoa
Intercept
Northwind
Sandspine
Thawnmouth
The Kingdom of Marcell
Whisp

regions/large-regions/western-haera.html.title: Western Haera
regions/large-regions/western-haera.html.description:
Western Haera is a region of vast plains, small forests with a small selection of mountain ranges, located in the west of Haera. It consists of:
Crekar
Drakehidt
Faeria
Gherra
Landspine
Lune
Raea
Windswept Plains

regions/small-regions/aethar.html.title: Aethar
regions/small-regions/aethar.html.description:
Aethar is a floating island in Outer Haera. The Haeran Battlemage Association is based here, and usually only members of the association are allowed to enter, along with Aethar's residents, who are primarily Aasimar. Establishments based in Aethar:
Haeran Battlemage Association

regions/small-regions/ascent.html.title: Ascent
regions/small-regions/ascent.html.description: Ascent is a mountain range in the Frostlands. Its largest mountain ascends into the clouds, earning its name.

regions/small-regions/blighted-lands.htm.title: Blighted Lands
regions/small-regions/blighted-lands.htm.description:
The Blighted Lands is a corrupted forest in Southern Haera. It is devoid of natural life, and the region is speculated to have experienced cross-plane bleed from the Shadowfell. A bizarre, inky black sky hovers over the region, with decay and death encompassing its woods.
Many academic schools have discouraged expeditions to the Blighted Lands, with the Institute of Natural Sciences being the sole academic school that obtained one of the only threads of information known about the region: it hosts some form of life.

regions/small-regions/blood-frosts.html.title: Blood Frosts
regions/small-regions/blood-frosts.html.description:
"If it wasn't for those magical particles, we'd have explored the whole island millennia ago." Professor Forbur Wilkinberry of The School of Ego,
The Blood Frosts is a snowy island in Outer Haera that consists of a large mountain range and purplish-red tints in the snow. The island frequently emits dangerous magical particles that are harmful to any who approach, thus halting research and investigation efforts.

regions/small-regions/canvas.html.title: Canvas
regions/small-regions/canvas.html.description:
"Home to plenty of magical kin, magical forests and a magical lake. Truly a remarkable geographical sight." Prof. Forbur Wilkinberry, The School of Ego
Canvas is a very large forest in Central Haera, bordering Stockold, Sandspine and Split. It is home to many elves, druids and other fantastical creatures.Canvas revolves around an enormous lake which is said to provide healing properties if processed correctly with the correct materials.
The Institute of Natural Sciences has sent numerous expeditions to Canvas, but often its participants find themselves charmed and removed from the forest, perhaps due to its inhabitants rejecting such rigorous research activity taking place.

regions/small-regions/chromatia.html.title: Chromatia
regions/small-regions/chromatia.html.description:
Chromatia is a ring of colorful islands in Outer Haera. It is established by The Circle of Ley Lines and the Institute of Natural Sciences that the islands emit magic in similar ways to Conduit.
However, it is widely agreed that Chromatia does not fall into Conduit Travel Theory, as calibrations and experiments regarding inter-planar travel are inconsistent and do not suggest any relation to the travel method described by the theory.

regions/small-regions/churn.html.title: Churn
regions/small-regions/churn.html.description: Churn is a desert region mostly surrounded by mountains in the north of Eastern Haera, consisting of the cities Spurn and Vortex.

regions/small-regions/conduit.html.title: Conduit
regions/small-regions/conduit.html.description:
Conduit is a group of mountains that emerge from a vast lake towards the west of Upper Central Haera.
Schools of wizardry, sorcery, churches and druids have studied Conduit for many years, and it is widely theorised that the mountains are host to very high magical energy, and if correct calibration and an inter-planar connection is made, it may be possible to harness the energy of Conduit to travel to other planes. See more in Conduit Travel Theory.

regions/small-regions/drakehidt.html.title: Drakehidt
regions/small-regions/drakehidt.html.description:
Drakehidt (pronounced Drake-height) is a peculiar mountain range in Western Haera that largely resembles dragon scales. A small selection of Dragonborn tribes, dwarves or halflings may be found living here.
The Circle of Ley Lines has previously investigated the mountains with Ley Lines, but were inconclusive on whether these scales originated from the ruins of a long-forgotten dragon, or some other beast.

regions/small-regions/egia-desert.html.title: Egia Desert
regions/small-regions/egia-desert.html.description: The Egia Desert is a large desert in Southern Haera. It is an extremely dry region, and temperature changes from day to night are very drastic.

regions/small-regions/ergo.html.title: Ergo
regions/small-regions/ergo.html.description:
Ergo is part of the Northern Mountains in Haera, stretching across the north of the continent. It lies to the east of Soarre and to the north of Snowspine.
It is speculated by the The School of Ego that the highest point in Ergo leads to the Upper Planes, or contains great compatibility for inter-planar travel.

regions/small-regions/eror.html.title: Eror
regions/small-regions/eror.html.description: Eror is a small dead forest in Southern Haera, where all life has been extinguished due to some unknown past event, speculated to have occurred long ago during The Forgotten Times.Attempts to restore life in the region have been made, but all of them failed. It remains a barren wasteland of skeletal trees and fossils.

regions/small-regions/exha.html.title: Exha
regions/small-regions/exha.html.description: Exha is a small mountain range in Northern Haera. It is infamous for being home to many dangerous monsters and beasts, as it hosts a vast array of hidden and largely unexplored cave systems within.

regions/small-regions/forsaken-perch.htm.title: Forsaken Perch
regions/small-regions/forsaken-perch.htm.description:
The Forsaken Perch is an impressive mountain range in Southern Haera boasting five extremely tall peaks, arranged in the shape of a '^'. Many Monks and Clerics have come to these mountains for training or worship, as its presence is believed to be enlightening or celestial by many.Small towns are built near the mountains as a result, and tourism is an advancing business for those wishing to gaze upon the mountains more closely. Establishments based in the Forsaken Perch:
Temples of the Divine
Monastery of Being

regions/small-regions/frostlands.html.title: Frostlands
regions/small-regions/frostlands.html.description: The Frostlands is a large pains with numerous mountain ranges in Northern Haera. Avador and Ascent lies within the Frostlands.

regions/small-regions/greyhorde.html.title: Greyhorde
regions/small-regions/greyhorde.html.description:
Greyhorde is a vast mountain range in Southern Haera. It is home to many dwarves and Dragonborn, with extensive underground communities and colonies built beneath its surface.
Due to the nature of such underground communities, documentation and research from outside sources in Haera are strictly prohibited, and thus academic schools have failed to provide further information about the mountains and its inhabitants.

regions/small-regions/intrigue.html.title: Intrigue
regions/small-regions/intrigue.html.description:
Intrigue is a mysterious island in Outer Haera. It boasts an odd geological appearance, with towering mountains and peaks surrounded by quaint palm trees. Generations of monks and philosophers have travelled to Intrigue to seek inner peace or training, similar to the Forsaken Perch.Its exotic palm trees produce various species of interesting fruit, often sold for very high prices due to its remoteness and exquisite taste.Establishments based in Intrigue:
Monastery of the Cloud

regions/small-regions/krag.html.title: Krag
regions/small-regions/krag.html.description:
"Truly spectacular. There is potential in these mountains for preserving life in an unorthodox manner." Professor Kahl Czekh, Institute of Natural Sciences
Krag is a small set of mountains in Northern Haera that exhibits strange ecological behavior, with deciduous trees remaining green in a small region around the mountains, despite the harsh cold weather all year long.
Researchers and scholars across Northern Haera have attempted to discern the cause and replicate the effects of Krag, but manually-placed nature do not exhibit the same effects as the pre-existing nature growing on the mountain. It is thought that this unbelievable nature is produced over many, many years of evolution under the effects of the mountain, and if removed, lose its connection to its powers completely.

regions/small-regions/landspine.html.title: Landspine
regions/small-regions/landspine.html.description: Landspine is a vast multi-directional mountain range that borders Western Haera, Central Haera and the mountains of Drakehidt.

regions/small-regions/lune.html.title: Lune
regions/small-regions/lune.html.description:
Lune is an inactive volcano in Western Haera accompanied by a small mountain range, with Raea to its north and the Windswept Plains to its east.
Though the volcano has not been active since The Warring Times, it is speculated by the Institute of Natural Sciences and The Circle of Ley Lines that the volcano may still erupt under unknown circumstances.

regions/small-regions/merphos.html.title: Merphos
regions/small-regions/merphos.html.description: Merphos is a vast forest in Eastern Haera home to many elves, druids, ....

regions/small-regions/northern-cities.ht.title: Northern Cities
regions/small-regions/northern-cities.ht.description:
The Northern Cities consist of various settlements in Northern Haera, including:
Dhor
Ego
Epiphany
Krag's Keep
Orhum
Reykja
Thawnia

regions/small-regions/northern-mountains.title: Northern Mountains
regions/small-regions/northern-mountains.description:
The Northern Mountains consist of various mountain ranges in the north of Haera, including:
Ascent
Ergo
Exha
Krag
Schmine
Snowspine
Soarre

regions/small-regions/northwind.html.title: Northwind
regions/small-regions/northwind.html.description:
Northwind is a region within Upper Central Haera, covering a significant portion of the area's cold forests. It contains numerous small settlements, towns and minor cities. Notable individuals:
The Northwind Wanderer Establishments based in Northwind:

regions/small-regions/obsidian-peaks.htm.title: Obsidian Peaks
regions/small-regions/obsidian-peaks.htm.description: The Obsidian Peaks are an array of violent volcanic mountains in Outer Haera, with remarkable height and power. As if some portraying an animal's defensive mechanism, attempts to reach and land on the Peaks have always failed as such expeditions are always met with sudden, unpredictable volcanic activity, forcing them to return to mainland Haera.

regions/small-regions/raea.html.title: Raea
regions/small-regions/raea.html.description:
Raea is a small mountain range in Western Haera, with Faeria to the east and Lune to the south.
Raea is a well-known hot springs attraction. Though the mountains make it infeasible to construct buildings, guided hikes and paths have been laid out to support tourism and visits from all over Haera.
The Institute of Natural Sciences, The Circle of Ley Lines and The School of Ego have confirmed with empirical evidence that the hot spring waters originating from Raea's geothermal sources produce subtle and refreshing healing qualities.

regions/small-regions/remnant.html.title: Remnant
regions/small-regions/remnant.html.description: Remnant is a small mountain range in the north of Southern Haera. It is rumored that extensive cave systems lie under it, but expeditions have not successfully identified a clear path down into it.

regions/small-regions/renavoir.html.title: Renavoir
regions/small-regions/renavoir.html.description: Renavoir is a rainforest situated in the south of Eastern Haera. It is home to many exotic animals, species and fruit. Its density and high humidity has made it difficult for cartographers to map out the region properly over the years, and many parts of it remain unexplored.Many adventurers have attempted to find hidden treasure in Renavoir, though thus far none have returned successful, or returned at all.

regions/small-regions/sandspine.html.title: Sandspine
regions/small-regions/sandspine.html.description: Sandspine is a vast mountain range that encompasses part of Churn and borders Merphos.

regions/small-regions/schmine.html.title: Schmine
regions/small-regions/schmine.html.description: Schmine is part of the Northern Mountains in Haera, stretching across the central north of the continent. It lies on the border of Northern Haera and Upper Central Haera.

regions/small-regions/snowspine.html.title: Snowspine
regions/small-regions/snowspine.html.description: Snowspine is part of the Northern Mountains in Haera, stretching across the north of the continent. It borders Ergo to the north and Schmine to the southwest.

regions/small-regions/soarre.html.title: Soarre
regions/small-regions/soarre.html.description: Soarre is part of the Northern Mountains in Haera, stretching across the north of the continent. It lies to the west of Ergo and to the north of Schmine.

regions/small-regions/split.html.title: Split
regions/small-regions/split.html.description:
Split is a small mountain range in Central Haera with a river that flows directly through the mountains.
It is rumored that in The Forgotten Times, a powerful demi-god or hero slashed through the mountain range with a powerful greatsword during a conflict.

regions/small-regions/stockold.html.title: Stockold
regions/small-regions/stockold.html.description: Stockold is a mountain range in Central Haera that is located near Canvas.

regions/small-regions/thawnmouth.html.title: Thawnmouth
regions/small-regions/thawnmouth.html.description: Thawnmouth is a set of mountains surrounding a lake in Upper Central Haera. Thawnia lies in its north.

regions/small-regions/windswept-plains.h.title: Windswept Plains
regions/small-regions/windswept-plains.h.description: The Windswept Plains are a large region of grassy plains in Western Haera, with Raea and Lune to the west.

religious-groups/the-church-of-grace.htm.title: The Church of Grace
religious-groups/the-church-of-grace.htm.description: The Church of Grace is a clergy primarily based in Temples of the Divine, but their ideals and teachings stretch far across Haera. They revere the grace and beauty of life and light, and gods who represent such ideals.

religious-groups/the-forgotten-church.ht.title: The Forgotten Church
religious-groups/the-forgotten-church.ht.description: The Forgotten Church is a clergy primarily based in Temples of the Divine, consisting of fractured remnants of past churches who worship gods, entities or beings long forgotten by the rest of Haera, or ones that are unconventional to worship. Though their worship is questionable by the public, their relations with other churches or groups such as The Holy Paladins or The Church of Grace are largely positive, as they are perceived as worshippers of 'other beings' rather than cryptic and strange cultists.

religious-groups/the-holy-paladins.html.title: The Holy Paladins
religious-groups/the-holy-paladins.html.description: The Holy Paladins are a group of Paladins primarily based in the Temples of the Divine. They do not worship the same gods, but they band together to carry out holy orders and protect the people of Haera. Due to the differing natures of different gods, there are factions within the group that largely operate separately, and often this could lead to inner conflicts and disagreements between beliefs and their actions.

religious-groups/the-oathbreakers.html.title: The Oathbreakers
religious-groups/the-oathbreakers.html.description: The Oathbreakers are a collection of Paladins who have broken their oath and work together to carry out their ideals, primarily based in the Temples of the Divine. Their nature conflicts with The Holy Paladins, and they often have disagreements when appearing in the same location. Though their gods have forsaken them, the Oathbreakers still find power within themselves to bring their vision of the future into reality.

species/aasimar.html.title: Aasimar
species/aasimar.html.description: Aasimar are humanoids who carry a spark of the Upper Planes within their souls. They can be descended from angelic beings or infused with Celestial power, fanning that spark to bring light, healing and heavenly fury.They can be of medium (4-7ft) or small size (2-4ft), with a speed of 30 feet.

species/dragonborn.html.title: Dragonborn
species/dragonborn.html.description: Dragonborn are humanoids that look like wingless, bipedal dragons, with horns on their heads. Their colorations and features are reminiscent of their draconic ancestors.They can be of medium size (5-7 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.Draconic Ancestors: Black Blue Brass Bronze Copper

species/dwarf.html.title: Dwarf
species/dwarf.html.description: Dwarves are resilient humanoids who have an affinity for stone, metal and living underground. They have a life span of around 350 years.They can be of medium size (4-5 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.

species/elf.html.title: Elf
species/elf.html.description: Elves are humanoids that may have been shaped by their environments over the years. They have a life span of around 750 years, and don't sleep.They can be of medium size (5-6 ft), with a speed of 30 feet (35 feet for wood elves).Elven Lineages: Drow High Elf Wood Elf

species/gnome.html.title: Gnome
species/gnome.html.description: Gnomes are clever humanoids who have pointed ears and big eyes. They have a life span of around 425 years.They can be of small size (3-4 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.

species/goliath.html.title: Goliath
species/goliath.html.description: Goliaths are humanoids who are descendants of giants. They can be of medium size (7-8 ft), with a speed of 35 feet.

species/halfling.html.title: Halfling
species/halfling.html.description: Halflings are humanoids who are very lucky. They have a life span of around 150 years.They can be of small size (2-3 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.

species/human.html.title: Human
species/human.html.description: Humans are diverse, ambitious and resourceful humanoids.They can be of medium (4-7 ft) or small size (2-4 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.

species/humanoid.html.title: Humanoid
species/humanoid.html.description:
Humanoids are a creature type, ranging from small sizes (2-4 feet tall) to medium (4-8 feet tall). Here are some humanoids in the land of Haera:
Aasimar
Dragonborn
Dwarf
Elf
Gnome
Goliath
Halfling
Human
Orc
Tiefling

species/orc.html.title: Orc
species/orc.html.description: Orcs are tall and broad humanoids with high endurance and determination.They can be of medium size (6-7 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.

species/tiefling.html.title: Tiefling
species/tiefling.html.description: Tieflings are humanoids who have fiendish ancestry. They have a life span of around 750 years, and don't sleep.They can be of medium (4-7 ft) or small size (3-4 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.Fiendish Legacies: Abyssal Chthonic Infernal

index.html.title: index
index.html.description: Welcome to the landing page of the Haera wiki!Get started by visiting Haera, or navigate through the wiki via the sidebar.

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.
